text: Fill in the blanks on amino acids, proteins, immunoglobulins, and haemoglobin.
questions: The mnemonic *PVT TIM HALL* helps remember the nine *essential/Essential* amino acids that must be obtained from the diet.
The four levels of protein structural organisation are *primary/Primary* (amino acid sequence), *secondary/Secondary* (alpha-helix and beta-sheet), tertiary, and *quaternary/Quaternary*.
Ammonia produced from amino acid catabolism is detoxified in the liver by the *urea cycle/Urea cycle/ornithine cycle*, and the rate-limiting enzyme is *carbamoyl phosphate synthetase I/CPS-I/CPS1*.
*Phenylketonuria/PKU* is caused by deficiency of the enzyme *phenylalanine hydroxylase/PAH*, leading to accumulation of *phenylalanine/Phe* and intellectual disability if untreated.
*IgG/Immunoglobulin G* is the most abundant immunoglobulin in serum, crosses the *placenta/Placenta*, and provides passive immunity to the newborn.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
